content: {"perspective": "Scientific", "question": "Round 1 answers surfaced that MetS-specific meta-analyses converge on direction but not on comparable point estimates, and that heterogeneity (I² up to 93.8%) is a dominant feature of the IF RCT literature. Which specific study-level moderators — IF protocol (TRE vs ADF vs 5:2 vs ICR), eating-window length, intervention duration, sex ratio, baseline HbA1c, concurrent pharmacotherapy — have meta-regression or subgroup analyses identified as sources of this heterogeneity, and which endpoints (FBS, HbA1c, HOMA-IR, LDL-C, IL-6, body weight) see the heterogeneity largely resolved after adjustment?", "search_queries": ["intermittent fasting meta-analysis heterogeneity I2 subgroup analysis moderators protocol duration", "\"time-restricted eating\" meta-regression eating window duration effect size heterogeneity", "intermittent fasting subgroup analysis sex baseline HbA1c moderator meta-analysis", "Zaman 2023 time-restricted eating subgroup analysis window length eating duration", "intermittent fasting ADF TRE 5:2 ICR pooled effect size subgroup comparison heterogeneity"]}
